action.reasoning: Observation: Best test PESQ remains 3.137 (MetricGAN+ + -25 LUFS norm, val PESQ flat at 2.862). ConvTasNet (1.2M params) after 10 epochs has train loss -14.21 but only val PESQ 2.026 (improving slowly from 1.83; best.pt saved). test_enhance confirms setup, training ~13s/epoch but full iter ~2h due to val+tests. Analysis: ConvTasNet underperforms MetricGAN+ (task-specific pretrained optimizing PESQ directly); val loop lacks loudness norm (used in predict/test) which may explain gap. More epochs/larger model/better scheduler/loss could help but time-tight (3.45h left, 13 iters). Root cause of low PESQ: limited capacity/training or domain gap. Decision: Extend test_enhance.py to load+eval current best ConvTasNet checkpoint (with/without loudness norm) on 100 val samples for direct comparison vs MetricGAN (expect ~2.1-2.4). If competitive, switch predict.py to it + submit_eval; else continue MetricGAN baseline + plan hybrid/HDemucs next. Fix train.py metrics.jsonl logging (currently malformed). Run test_enhance to diagnose before longer training.
action.command: python test_enhance.py && echo 'Test enhance completed, now checking logs' && ls -R
